name: Fix it from Aurtitz
region_intro: Hiking around Ituren offers exploration within the Pyrenean foothills of northern Navarra. The region is characterized by a mountainous backdrop, extensive pastures, and forests of oak, chestnut, walnut, beech, and ash. Ituren is situated on the flood plain of the River Ezkurra within the Malerreka Valley, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. The landscape includes verdant fields and dry-stone walls, with Mendaur mountain (1,060 meters) dominating the skyline. Best hiking trails around Ituren The most popular hiking route is Fix it from Aurtitz, a 6.5 miles (10.4 km) trail that takes 5 hours to complete, featuring significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Uraren Bidea - Water Path, a moderate 9.3 miles (15.0 km) path. This route follows the "Water Path," offering a relatively flat journey along the river with minimal elevation changes. Local hikers also love the Mendaur Summit and Hermitage – MenhirDeLearra loop from Ituren, a 7.3 miles (11.8 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain to the summit of Mendaur, often completed in about 5 hours 10 minutes. Ituren hiking terrain is defined by Pyrenean foothills, river valleys, and forested mountain slopes. The network of trails offers options for various ability levels, from moderate valley walks to more challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Ituren are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from 200 reviews. More than 1600 hikers have used komoot to explore Ituren's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of hiking terrain can I expect around Ituren?
A: Ituren's hiking terrain is diverse, ranging from the Pyrenean foothills with mountainous slopes to the floodplains of the River Ezkurra in the Malerreka Valley. You'll find extensive pastures, orchards, and forests of oak, chestnut, walnut, beech, and ash. Trails can vary from relatively flat river paths, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970209460/uraren-bidea-water-path" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Uraren Bidea - Water Path&lt;/a&gt;, to challenging ascents up mountains like Mendaur.

Q: Are there many hiking trails available in Ituren?
A: Yes, there are over 90 hiking routes around Ituren, catering to various skill levels. This includes 11 easy routes, 56 moderate routes, and 24 difficult routes, ensuring options for everyone from casual walkers to experienced trekkers.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Ituren?
A: The trails around Ituren are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from 200 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning natural landscapes, the variety of terrain, and the rewarding views from mountain summits like Mendaur.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options near Ituren?
A: Yes, Ituren offers several easy and moderate routes suitable for families. While specific family-designated trails aren't listed, routes with minimal elevation changes, such as sections of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970209460/uraren-bidea-water-path" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Uraren Bidea - Water Path&lt;/a&gt;, would be good choices for a leisurely walk with children.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes around Ituren?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15004012" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mendaur Summit and Hermitage – MenhirDeLearra loop from Ituren&lt;/a&gt; offers a challenging circular hike to the summit of Mendaur, providing panoramic views.

Q: What natural landmarks or attractions can I see while hiking near Ituren?
A: While hiking near Ituren, you can explore several natural attractions. The region is dominated by Mendaur mountain, featuring the white-washed chapel of La Trinidad on its summit. You can also discover beautiful reservoirs like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5185050" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Leurza Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5230374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;EmbalseArritzulegi&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5840990" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bisusto Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is another natural highlight worth visiting.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints along the trails in Ituren?
A: Many trails in Ituren offer rewarding viewpoints, particularly those ascending Mendaur mountain. The summit of Mendaur, for instance, provides extensive panoramic views of the Pyrenean foothills and the surrounding Malerreka Valley. The Mirador del Valle de Baztan, though a short drive away, also offers excellent vistas of the wider region.

Q: Is public transport available to reach trailheads around Ituren?
A: Ituren is a traditional Basque village, and while local bus services might connect it to larger towns in Navarra, direct public transport to specific remote trailheads may be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider personal transport for greater flexibility in accessing various starting points.

Q: Are there any permits required for hiking in the Ituren area?
A: Generally, permits are not required for hiking on established trails in the Ituren area or the Pyrenean foothills of Navarra. However, if you plan to enter specific protected areas or private lands, it's always best to check local regulations. For general information on hiking in Navarra, you can visit &lt;a href="https://www.visitnavarra.es/en/hiking" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitnavarra.es&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike around Ituren?
A: The Pyrenean foothills around Ituren offer pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn provide mild temperatures and vibrant scenery with blooming flowers or changing foliage. Summer can be warm but offers long daylight hours, while winter hiking is possible, though higher elevations may experience snow and require appropriate gear.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly trails in Ituren?
A: Many of the natural trails around Ituren are suitable for dogs, especially those through pastures and forests. It's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash, particularly in areas with livestock or near protected wildlife. Always ensure you carry water for your dog and clean up after them.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Ituren?
A: Parking is typically available within Ituren village itself, often near the main square or public areas. For specific trailheads, like those for Mendaur or the Uraren Bidea, you may find designated parking spots or roadside parking options closer to the starting points. It's advisable to check the specific route details on komoot for parking recommendations.
tips: Mount Mendaur is located in the Malerreka valley, in the Navarran pre-Pyrenees. It is next to the towns of Sunbilla and Ituren mainly.
